reports.title: Trump Eyes New Iran Strikes as Oil Hits $126
reports.subtitle: US Central Command prepares a fresh wave of strikes while a fragile ceasefire is still technically in place.

reports.title: SCOTUS Strikes Down Louisiana Map, Gutting Voting Rights Act
reports.subtitle: The court rules majority-minority districts unconstitutional, handing Republicans a redistricting weapon heading into the midterms.

reports.title: Musk Takes the Stand Against OpenAI, Claims Altman Stole a Charity
reports.subtitle: Day three of the trial reveals a fight not about AI safety but about who controls the most valuable organization in Silicon Valley.

reports.title: Powell's Last Stand: The Fed Holds Rates and Its Ground
reports.subtitle: Jerome Powell holds rates steady in what is expected to be his final meeting as Fed chair, warning that legal attacks are battering the institution.

reports.title: Big Tech Earns More, Spends More on AI. Meta Investors Revolt.
reports.subtitle: Alphabet's 63% cloud growth contrasts with Meta's share drop after raising AI capex to $145 billion with no clear payoff plan.

reports.title: UAE Quits OPEC After 60 Years, and Saudi Arabia Has No Answer
reports.subtitle: Abu Dhabi's defection doesn't change oil prices today, but it signals that the cartel's era of shared discipline is over.

reports.title: Israel Seizes Gaza Aid Flotilla 1,000 Miles From Gaza
reports.subtitle: The interception near Crete ends the mission before it starts and turns 175 detained activists into a diplomatic problem for every government that said nothing.

reports.title: Kevin Warsh Clears Senate Banking Committee. The Fed's Independence Window Is Closing.
reports.subtitle: The party-line 13-11 vote advances a nominee who pledged Fed independence but whose confirmation is a direct reward for Trump's months-long pressure campaign against Powell.

reports.title: Trump Pays $2 Billion in Taxpayer Money to Kill Wind Farms
reports.subtitle: The administration is using federal funds to buy out offshore wind contracts it could not cancel legally, turning a policy reversal into a taxpayer liability.

reports.title: Saudi Arabia Is Cutting LIV Golf Loose. The Sportswashing Bet Failed.
reports.subtitle: After three years and billions spent buying defections and legitimacy, Riyadh is walking away from a tour that never became a sport.
